meaning: n.[解]扁桃形结构,如扁桃体
examples: The blurred imagery of Impressionist paintings seems to stimulate the brain's amygdala.
When people uttered a falsehood, the scientists noticed a burst of activity in their amygdala.
The amygdala receives input from many parts of the brain, including regions responsible for retrieving memories.
In addition, amygdala volumes did not correlate with any measure of drug use in the addicts.
As the oldest part of the brain, the amygdala prioritizes survival and regulates the fight-or-flight response.
